overview: Dimir Reanimator is a Standard archetype that blends midrange disruption with an unfair reanimation combo to cheat large threats onto the battlefield. It leverages graveyard synergy and threshold enablers like Kiora, the Rising Tide to efficiently cast Zombify and Nullpriest of Oblivion. While its primary goal is to reanimate Atraxa, Grand Unifier or Valgavoth, Terror Eater, it maintains a resilient Plan B through token generation and flexible removal.
win_conditions: Reanimating Atraxa, Grand Unifier for card advantage and board presence
Reanimating Valgavoth, Terror Eater to generate value from opponent's removal
Winning with Kiora, the Rising Tide token generation as a Plan B

keep_criteria: Hands with cheap reanimation spells like Zombify
Hands containing enablers like Kiora, the Rising Tide or Evangel of Synthesis
Hands with early interaction and lands
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking reanimation targets or spells
Hands without early game disruption or filtering
Hands with too many high-cost bombs without enablers

examples.reason: Provides filtering, early interaction, a reanimation spell, and a target to execute the plan quickly.
Lacks cheap reanimation or early filtering, making it difficult to reach the combo on curve.

early_game: Filter draws with Kiora, the Rising Tide and Evangel of Synthesis
Play cheap removal like Cut Down and Anoint with Affliction to stabilize
Dump cards into the graveyard to enable Zombify and threshold
mid_game: Cast reanimation spells like Zombify and Nullpriest of Oblivion to bring back threats
Use Overlord of the Balemurk to recur enablers and reanimation spells
Apply pressure with reanimated creatures or Kiora tokens
late_game: Win with board presence from Atraxa or Valgavoth
Use sideboard answers like Malicious Eclipse or Withering Torment to clear wide boards or enchantments
Leverage card advantage from reanimated threats to outvalue opponents

key_cards.name: Zombify
key_cards.notes: Cheapest reanimation effect, most linear; priority is big creatures but can be used on Kiora or Overlord.

key_cards.name: Atraxa, Grand Unifier
key_cards.notes: Primary target against Aggro, combo, or White Tokens; generates card advantage and board presence.

key_cards.name: Valgavoth, Terror Eater
key_cards.notes: Primary target against Midrange; generates value from opponent's spot removal.

key_cards.name: Kiora, the Rising Tide
key_cards.notes: Filters draws, triggers Threshold, and creates tokens for a Plan B if the board is empty.

key_cards.name: Overlord of the Balemurk
key_cards.notes: Recurs Kiora or Nullpriest of Oblivion from the graveyard, adding resilience to the game plan.

key_cards.name: Nullpriest of Oblivion
key_cards.notes: Six-mana reanimation effect that can be recur with Overlord; provides a 2/1 lifelink body early.

key_cards.name: Virtue of Persistence
key_cards.notes: Expensive reanimation but functions as cheap early removal; cast for seven mana later in the game.

key_cards.name: Bitter Triumph
key_cards.notes: Efficient response against creatures and Planeswalkers; synergizes by discarding cards to the graveyard.

key_cards.name: Duress
key_cards.notes: Protects the combo from counterspells and finds reanimation targets or threats.

key_cards.name: Undercity Sewers
key_cards.notes: Filters the top of the deck and puts reanimation targets in the graveyard or feeds Kiora.

sequencing_tips: Use Duress early to protect your reanimation spells from counterspells or to find targets.
Prioritize reanimating Atraxa or Valgavoth with Zombify, but consider reanimating Kiora or Overlord if you need immediate filtering or recursion.
Attack with Overlord of the Balemurk to trigger its ability and return Kiora or Nullpriest of Oblivion to your hand.
Use Bitter Triumph to discard cards to the graveyard while efficiently removing opponent's threats.
Save counterspells like Negate or Disdainful Stroke for spells that prevent your combo or for opponent's answers to your reanimated creatures.

matchups.advice: Use cheap removal and Duress to disrupt their early game. Reanimate threats quickly to outpace their aggression.
Target Valgavoth, Terror Eater as your primary reanimation target to generate value from their removal. Use Withering Torment for larger creatures.
Side in Malicious Eclipse to handle their wide board. Use Duress to find reanimation targets and protect your combo.
Side in Ghost Vacuum to exile their threats and protect your graveyard. Use counterspells to disrupt their tempo plays.
Use early removal and reanimate threats to stabilize. Leverage Kiora's token plan if the board clears.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Empowered by the inclusion of Zombify in Foundations and the ability of Valgavoth, Terror Eater to take over games... The rest of the deck is classic Midrange with cheap removal and disruption... On the one hand, we have an unfair plan that wins games for free... since we require them for the “combo” to work.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Atraxa, Grand Unifier is best in games where we don't need to protect our threats and/or where we want to generate a lot of card advantage.
Valgavoth, Terror Eater excels in games where the opponent has a lot of spot removal while also generating card advantage with the opponent's cards
The token created by her wins the game in a few turns if we keep the board empty

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Sometimes it's worth reanimating Kiora, The Rising Tide or Overlord of the Balemurk with it, but the priority should be on the big creatures.
its attack trigger and ETB ensure that we can always return Kiora or Nullpriest of Oblivion to our hand
The two copies of Duress in the maindeck guarantee protection against counterspells when we want to execute the combo
our best targets for counterspells are the spells that prevent our combo from working, but we should also consider the types of responses our opponents may have after we reanimate a creature.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: presence of Jeskai Convoke and Azorius Aggro in the current Metagame
Blot Out is our answer to Kaito, Bane of Nightmares , but it can also be used against Domain Ramp and/or other archetypes where Hexproof is relevant, such as in the mirror or against Four-Color Zur.
Affliction also has the advantage of dealing with Unstoppable Slasher and Enduring Innocence permanently.
answers troublesome enchantments like Unholy Annex and Leyline Binding . It is an essential piece in games where we expect Rest in Peace or Leyline of the Void .
